lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_coercivity_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation boundary portion partial Sigma_R, the sign-controlled boundary flux pairing supplies the required coercive boundary control whenever the explicitly listed coercivity constant, trace-norm compatibility, and restricted-boundary support hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_sign_control_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the already certified sign control for the boundary flux pairing

required_inputs.name: boundary_flux_coercivity_constant_positive
required_inputs.role: supplies the positive constant needed for coercive boundary control

required_inputs.name: boundary_trace_norm_matches_energy_boundary_norm
required_inputs.role: identifies the trace norm controlled by the flux with the boundary norm used in the energy estimate

required_inputs.name: coercive_flux_lower_bound_available
required_inputs.role: supplies the lower-bound inequality converting sign control into quantitative coercivity

required_inputs.name: restricted_boundary_support_contains_only_coercive_faces
required_inputs.role: ensures every contributing restricted-boundary face satisfies the coercivity hypothesis

discharged_obligations: Separates boundary flux coercivity from boundary flux sign control.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation boundary flux pairing gives quantitative coercive control.
Promotes boundary flux coercivity from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove restricted integration by parts.
Does not prove the energy inequality.
Does not prove continuation or contradiction.
Does not prove full RR closure.
